# UC-13.3.4 · Cross-Tool Monitoring Coverage Gap, Blind-Spot Host Detection, Sensor Fleet Inventory Reconciliation, and CMDB-versus-Telemetry Drift Forensics

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Expert &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Configuration, Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We compare the official machine list from your inventory systems with the signals we actually receive so we can spot missing collectors, unexpected senders, and half-finished setups before audits or outages surprise leadership.*

---

## Description

Detects CMDB and enterprise inventory drift against live Splunk telemetry by correlating ServiceNow, Workday, IPAM, and cloud-tag inventory rows with multisearch-derived receipt freshness across tstats operational indexes, OpenTelemetry agent inventories, Universal Forwarder tcpin_connections, and cloud audit lanes; flags blind-spot hosts with no or stale events, rogue senders absent from inventory, partial stream coverage against role expectations, environment-skewed coverage ratios, sensor-fleet scale mismatches, and directional cloud-account receipt gaps for governance forensics.

## Value

Risk, platform, and audit stakeholders gain a single reconciled narrative that proves which declared assets are silent, which telemetry sources lack ownership, and which roles miss mandated streams before regulators or customers discover the gap; operational leaders shrink mean time to restore instrumentation while security teams prioritize unauthorized senders with evidence grounded in inventory truth.

## Implementation

Land nightly cmdb_master_inventory.csv and expected_telemetry_streams_by_role.csv lookups; ingest OTel agent inventory to index=otel_agents; keep tstats-accelerated operational indexes and _internal tcpin_connections; save observability_uc_13_3_4_cmdb_telemetry_coverage_reconciliation every thirty minutes over earliest=-24h@h; route critical and high severities to platform inventory on-call; archive weekly evidence CSV exports to a restricted index.

## Evidence

Saved search observability_uc_13_3_4_cmdb_telemetry_coverage_reconciliation with thirty-minute schedule; weekly CSV exports to a restricted evidence index; dashboard drilldowns on multisearch lanes and cmdb_master_inventory.csv joins.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab host marked in_service in cmdb_master_inventory.csv with production environment tags, stop the Universal Forwarder and OTel agent for longer than the blind-spot window while keeping CMDB unchanged; confirm observability_uc_13_3_4_cmdb_telemetry_coverage_reconciliation raises critical or medium blind_spot within two scheduled runs.

### Negative scenario

After restoring agents, with matching expected_streams coverage and fresh last_seen_telemetry across lanes, confirm severities return to info or low for that host and the alert wrapper emits no critical or high rows for thirty consecutive schedules.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the configuration-management team that curates ServiceNow CMDB or equivalent golden inventory, the identity and IPAM owners who publish authoritative DNS and address allocations, the cloud-finops group that exports AWS Organizations, Azure subscription, and Google Cloud billing resource inventories, and the observability engineers who operate Universal Forwarders, OpenTelemetry Collectors, syslog aggregators, SNMP pollers, and NetFlow collectors feeding Splunk. This use case is deliberately about reconciliation between declared assets in enterprise inventory systems and the hosts, instances, and appliances that actually emit telemetry into Splunk indexes within a defined freshness window. It answers whether production servers that the business believes are instrumented are silent, whether unknown senders are flooding indexes without a matching inventory row, whether partially instrumented roles are missing mandated streams such as Windows Security alongside perfmon, whether coverage ratios differ by environment in ways that violate policy, whether forwarder or OTel agent counts drift from declared Linux and Windows populations, and whether cloud-account activity volume aligns with billed resource counts for governance spot checks.

Scope boundaries keep this UC distinct from sibling cat-13.3 observability integrations. UC-13.3.1 remains legacy Nagios, Zabbix, Icinga, Nimsoft, and SCOM alert-ingest deduplication and heartbeat silence across classic NMS lanes rather than CMDB-to-telemetry identity reconciliation. UC-13.3.2 remains Prometheus scrape-target and remote-write health rather than inventory coverage. UC-13.3.3 remains PagerDuty and Opsgenie routing semantics rather than asset completeness. UC-13.3.5 remains alert-storm economics and paging deduplication rather than silent hosts versus inventory. UC-13.3.7 remains distributed-trace sampling yield and bias detection rather than host-level telemetry receipt coverage. UC-13.3.15 remains OpenTelemetry Collector throughput and queue pressure rather than whether every CMDB server has an agent installed. Splunk becomes the neutral ground where nightly CMDB extracts, Workday-owned asset feeds, IPAM exports, cloud tag snapshots, and live tstats receipt timestamps meet in one auditable correlation.

RBAC and data minimization matter. Read-only database or Table API accounts should pull CMDB extracts without interactive admin credentials on analyst laptops. HTTP Event Collector tokens for cloud audit mirrors must rotate on a published schedule. Restrict cmdb_master_inventory.csv and expected_telemetry_streams_by_role.csv to roles that already handle asset data; hash or truncate free-text owner fields when legal counsel requires minimization. Separate production and non-production Splunk indexes so developers experimenting with agents do not pollute production coverage ratios without explicit tagging.

Index design: land normalized CMDB snapshots into a restricted index family such as index=cmdb_inventory with sourcetype=cmdb:servicenow:computer or cmdb:workday:asset when transforms normalize JSON. Operational telemetry belongs in index=main, index=os, index=wineventlog, index=infra, index=network, index=k8s, index=netops, or your standardized equivalents. OpenTelemetry agent posture belongs in index=otel_agents when a modular input or scheduled exporter writes one row per agent with version and configuration hash. Cloud audit trails for account coverage spot checks belong in index=aws_cloudtrail, index=azure_aad, index=gcp_audit, or index=security when your ES or SOC team already centralizes those feeds. Universal Forwarder attachment evidence belongs in index=_internal with sourcetype=splunkd and group=tcpin_connections for connection-oriented views of forwarders speaking to indexers. Retain at least fourteen days hot for operational replay and thirty days for compliance attestations.

Field normalization: treat host keys as lowercased strings coalesced from host, hostname, fqdn, primary_dns, dest, and agent_host depending on the lane. Map cmdb_class, cmdb_owner, cmdb_status, host_role, host_environment, business_unit, compliance_zone, and geo_region from cmdb_master_inventory.csv without renaming away from analyst-friendly labels expected in governance reports. Preserve last_seen_telemetry as the maximum _time observed across multisearch arms so a single stale lane does not erase evidence that another lane is healthy. Encode expected_streams as a pipe-delimited catalog aligned to expected_telemetry_streams_by_role.csv so Windows database servers, Linux web tiers, network appliances, and Kubernetes worker nodes each carry explicit stream expectations rather than implicit folklore.

Risk briefing: a quiet host during an approved maintenance freeze should not page if maintenance_window_id or change_ticket_id annotations flow through lookups; tune blind_spot thresholds per environment so production uses tighter hours than development. Rogue_asset rows often trace to ephemeral lab devices, contractor laptops, or mis-tagged cloud instances; require dual evidence from DNS and IPAM before declaring incident-level unauthorized access. Partial_stream_prod detections depend on honest expected_streams lists; if the catalog lags architecture decisions, Splunk will cry wolf until GitOps updates the CSV. Cloud audit versus billing comparisons are directional only; invoice APIs and activity logs differ in granularity and delay.

Collector posture: stamp deployment_group and build_id on OTel and forwarder payloads so partial_stream investigations can tie missing streams to a bad Ansible role or image version. When tcpin_connections shows attachment but tstats shows no user data indexes, suspect routing or index-time filtering rather than a dead host.

Differentiation recap: CMDB and inventory sources of truth correlated to Splunk telemetry receipt freshness, stream completeness, environment-tiered coverage ratios, forwarder and OTel fleet scale checks, and optional cloud audit presence—not legacy NMS dedupe, not Prometheus scrape health, not paging vendor storms, not trace sampling math, not collector queue saturation in isolation.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane A projects generalized telemetry freshness with tstats latest(_time) BY host across index=main, index=os, index=wineventlog, index=infra, index=network, index=k8s, and index=netops using your accelerated summaries where available. Restrict to production-forwarding classes when testing first waves; widen indexes only after Job Inspector proves median runtimes stay inside service levels. Ensure host fields are indexed and not solely indexed-time tags so tstats arms return rows.

Lane B lands OpenTelemetry agent inventory snapshots in index=otel_agents with sourcetype=otel:agent:inventory or equivalent, carrying host, agent_host, otel_host, service_instance_id, agent_version, and config_hash. Schedule the exporter every fifteen minutes during walk phase and every hour in steady state if API budgets require it.

Lane C reads index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd group=tcpin_connections for Universal Forwarder attachment signals, coalescing hostname and sourceHost into the same canonical host key used by CMDB. Pair with Monitoring Console forwarder lists for human validation but keep Splunk search as the automated source.

Lane D uses tstats latest(_time) BY host against index=aws_cloudtrail, index=azure_aad, index=gcp_audit, or index=security for cloud-account coverage forensics where host or instance identifiers appear as dest or host in your CIM mappings. This arm is not a full FinOps reconciliation; it supplies a receipt signal that billed-shaped identities are generating audit-like events inside Splunk.

Maintain cmdb_master_inventory.csv with columns host, hostname, fqdn, primary_dns, host_role, host_environment, cmdb_class, cmdb_owner, cmdb_status, business_unit, compliance_zone, geo_region, cloud_account_id, and last_cmdb_refresh_epoch refreshed from ServiceNow transform maps, Workday exports, IPAM, and cloud tag inventories. Maintain expected_telemetry_streams_by_role.csv with host_role, expected_streams pipe lists, min_prod_coverage_pct for ratio governance, and notes referencing architecture standards.

props.conf and transforms.conf should normalize time zones to UTC at ingest for cross-region fairness. Use a dedicated summary index materializing per-host last_seen hourly if multisearch cost grows; point alerts at summaries and keep this full search for investigations.

Example readiness checks from a hardened bastion: verify ServiceNow export row counts match CMDB UI filtered views, curl OTel collector health endpoints for agent lists, and compare Splunk Monitoring Console forwarder count to tcpin distinct hosts within five percent during steady state.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_3_4_cmdb_telemetry_coverage_reconciliation with a thirty-minute schedule in production, dispatch.earliest_time=-24h@h, dispatch.latest_time=now, and alert when severity is critical or high after suppressing maintenance-tagged hosts via a lookup join you maintain separately. Throttle duplicate host rows for forty-five minutes unless severity escalates. Include gap_type, coverage_ratio, and recommended_action in pager narratives so responders open CMDB, forwarder deployment, or OTel Ansible roles without rebuilding joins during incidents.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans four lanes—general tstats freshness, OTel agent inventory, Universal Forwarder tcpin attachment, and cloud-audit tstats—so a silent OTel CSV cannot hide healthy forwarder traffic or vice versa. coalesce() ladders normalize host identity across exporters. streamstats with a two-event window per host highlights jitter between consecutive receipts for forensic timelines in investigations. stats collapses duplicate host rows per lane before joins. eventstats adds fleet_stream_median for context in dashboards and env_avg_coverage BY host_environment for ratio anomalies. join type=left max=0 host pulls cmdb_master_inventory.csv fields. join type=left max=0 host_role pulls expected_telemetry_streams_by_role.csv expectations. case() encodes gap_type for blind_spot, rogue_asset, partial_stream_prod, partial_stream_nonprod, or covered_or_review. A second case() block maps severity to critical, high, medium, low, or info with production tightening. recommended_action stays verb-oriented for runbook hyperlinks.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.3.4 CMDB inventory vs telemetry blind-spot rogue partial-stream coverage earliest=-24h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ | tstats latest(_time) AS ts WHERE (index=main OR index=os OR index=wineventlog OR index=infra OR index=network OR index=k8s OR index=netops) earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY host
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval lane="tstats_telemetry_freshness"
      | eval last_seen_telemetry=ts ]
    [ search index=otel_agents earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,agent_host,otel_host,""))))
      | where len(host)>1
      | stats latest(_time) AS ts BY host
      | eval lane="otel_agent_inventory"
      | eval last_seen_telemetry=ts ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd group=tcpin_connections earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(hostname,sourceHost,host,""))))
      | where len(host)>1
      | stats latest(_time) AS ts BY host
      | eval lane="splunk_uf_tcpin"
      | eval last_seen_telemetry=ts ]
    [ | tstats latest(_time) AS ts WHERE (index=aws_cloudtrail OR index=azure_aad OR index=gcp_audit OR index=security) earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY host
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval lane="tstats_cloud_audit_lane"
      | eval last_seen_telemetry=ts ]
| eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,""))))
| where len(host)>1
| sort 0 host - last_seen_telemetry
| streamstats window=2 global=f current=f last(last_seen_telemetry) AS prev_seen BY host
| stats max(last_seen_telemetry) AS last_seen_telemetry values(lane) AS lane_mv max(prev_seen) AS dwell_hint BY host
| eval actual_streams=mvjoin(mvsort(mvdedup(lane_mv)),"|")
| eval stream_dc=mvcount(mvsort(mvdedup(lane_mv)))
| eventstats median(stream_dc) AS fleet_stream_median
| join type=left max=0 host
    [| inputlookup cmdb_master_inventory.csv
     | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,fqdn,primary_dns,""))))
     | fields host host_role host_environment cmdb_class cmdb_owner cmdb_status business_unit compliance_zone geo_region ]
| join type=left max=0 host_role
    [| inputlookup expected_telemetry_streams_by_role.csv
     | eval host_role=lower(trim(toString(host_role)))
     | fields host_role expected_streams min_prod_coverage_pct ]
| eval host_role=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host_role,"unknown_role"))))
| eval host_environment=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host_environment,"unknown"))))
| eval cmdb_class=coalesce(cmdb_class,"")
| eval cmdb_owner=coalesce(cmdb_owner,"")
| eval cmdb_status=coalesce(cmdb_status,"")
| eval business_unit=coalesce(business_unit,"unknown_bu")
| eval compliance_zone=coalesce(compliance_zone,"unknown_zone")
| eval geo_region=coalesce(geo_region,"unknown_region")
| eval in_cmdb=if(len(cmdb_class)>0 OR len(cmdb_owner)>0,1,0)
| eval expected_streams=coalesce(expected_streams,"tstats_telemetry_freshness|splunk_uf_tcpin|otel_agent_inventory")
| eval exp_parts=split(expected_streams,"|")
| eval exp_cnt=mvcount(exp_parts)
| eval coverage_ratio=if(exp_cnt>0, round(100*stream_dc/exp_cnt,2), null())
| eval gap_age_h=if(isnotnull(last_seen_telemetry), round((now()-last_seen_telemetry)/3600,3), null())
| eval gap_type=case(in_cmdb==1 AND (isnull(last_seen_telemetry) OR gap_age_h>24),"blind_spot", in_cmdb==0 AND isnotnull(last_seen_telemetry),"rogue_asset", in_cmdb==1 AND isnotnull(coverage_ratio) AND coverage_ratio<100 AND match(host_environment,"prod|production"),"partial_stream_prod", in_cmdb==1 AND isnotnull(coverage_ratio) AND coverage_ratio<100,"partial_stream_nonprod", true(),"covered_or_review")
| eventstats avg(coverage_ratio) AS env_avg_coverage BY host_environment
| eval severity=case(
    gap_type=="blind_spot" AND match(host_environment,"prod|production"), "critical",
    gap_type=="rogue_asset" AND stream_dc>=2, "high",
    gap_type=="partial_stream_prod", "high",
    gap_type=="blind_spot", "medium",
    gap_type=="partial_stream_nonprod", "low",
    gap_type=="rogue_asset", "medium",
    true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    gap_type=="blind_spot", "restore_agents_forwarders_syslog_or_fix_dns_host_alias_for_inventory_key",
    gap_type=="rogue_asset", "classify_host_in_cmdb_or_quarantine_unauthorized_telemetry_source",
    gap_type=="partial_stream_prod" OR gap_type=="partial_stream_nonprod", "deploy_missing_streams_from_expected_telemetry_streams_by_role",
    true(), "maintain_weekly_inventory_reconciliation_runbook")
| table host host_role host_environment cmdb_class cmdb_owner cmdb_status last_seen_telemetry expected_streams actual_streams coverage_ratio business_unit compliance_zone geo_region severity gap_type recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_3_4_cmdb_telemetry_coverage_reconciliation_alert]
cron_schedule = */30 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -24h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-inventory-oncall@example.com
action.email.subject = CMDB telemetry gap $result.severity$ $result.host$ $result.gap_type$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_3_4_cmdb_telemetry_coverage_reconciliation | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance: if multisearch queue time exceeds objectives, precompute a telemetry_last_seen summary index keyed on host and lane, widen alerts to earliest=-6h@h against summaries, and retain this search for deep dives. For very large fleets, filter cmdb_master_inventory.csv to in-scope compliance zones before joining.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in ServiceNow CMDB or your authoritative inventory UI: pick ten hosts stratified by environment and confirm cmdb_status and host_role match cmdb_master_inventory.csv within one refresh cycle. When Splunk shows blind_spot but the device is decommissioned, fix CMDB lag before muting Splunk.

Forwarder validation: compare Monitoring Console forwarder list to tcpin_connections distinct hosts and to tstats hosts for the same day. When tcpin shows attachment but tstats shows no data indexes, validate outputs.conf routing, index allow lists, and filter macros.

OTel validation: compare index=otel_agents host lists to your configuration-management database of installed collectors. When agents exist but OTel index is quiet, validate HEC tokens and intermediate gateways.

Partial stream validation: for a Windows production SQL host, confirm Windows Event Log security, perfmon, and forwarder metadata lanes all appear in actual_streams when expected_streams demands them; inject a controlled gap in non-production and confirm partial_stream_prod classification within two runs.

Cloud spot check: compare distinct cloud audit hosts to a small sample of billing-exported instance identifiers manually; document delay and cardinality limits before executive reporting.

Synthetic negative test: after recovery, filtering severity to critical and high should return zero rows for thirty consecutive schedules when inventory and telemetry are aligned.

Closing validation artifact: archive weekly CSV exports of critical and high rows to a restricted evidence index with reviewer initials.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Production blind spot with live CMDB status: validate Universal Forwarder service state, firewall paths, and certificate trust to indexers; restore deployment server phone-home before clearing the incident.

Case 2 — Rogue asset with strong multi-lane telemetry: trace source IP and sourcetype mix, coordinate with security operations for possible shadow IT, and either onboard CMDB or block unauthorized tokens.

Case 3 — Partial Windows stream missing security events while perfmon flows: repair inputs.conf Windows Event Log stanzas, validate XML channel permissions, and confirm GPO did not disable auditing.

Case 4 — Partial Linux stream missing syslog after image hardening: restore rsyslog or systemd-journal forwarding, validate SELinux contexts, and re-test with logger injections.

Case 5 — OTel agent inventory stale while data indexes show OTLP spans: fix exporter scheduling, validate index routing for inventory sourcetype, and dedupe duplicate agent_host aliases.

Case 6 — tcpin attachment without user indexes: repair outputs.conf defaultGroup, server lists, and cluster discovery; confirm the host is not a deployment server only.

Case 7 — Cloud audit lane quiet with active billing growth: validate CloudTrail organization trail, Azure diagnostic settings, or GCP audit sink configuration before claiming coverage.

Case 8 — CMDB duplicate FQDN versus short name splits coverage: rebuild cmdb_master_inventory.csv primary_dns keys and normalize host aliases in transforms.

Case 9 — Kubernetes node counted as bare metal in CMDB: introduce node_role tags, split kubelet-forwarded hosts from physical asset classes, and tune expected_streams for worker versus control-plane roles.

Case 10 — NetFlow-only presence without SNMP for network class: update expected_telemetry_streams_by_role.csv for edge routers until SNMP returns.

Case 11 — Compliance-zone suppression misfire: fix lookup joins so EU-regulated hosts still page during blind_spot while sandbox zones warn only.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists four arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, inputlookup, and tstats appear; final table lists sixteen analyst-visible columns including host, host_role, host_environment, cmdb_class, cmdb_owner, cmdb_status, last_seen_telemetry, expected_streams, actual_streams, coverage_ratio, business_unit, compliance_zone, geo_region, severity, gap_type, and recommended_action.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.3.4 CMDB inventory vs telemetry blind-spot rogue partial-stream coverage earliest=-24h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ | tstats latest(_time) AS ts WHERE (index=main OR index=os OR index=wineventlog OR index=infra OR index=network OR index=k8s OR index=netops) earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY host
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval lane="tstats_telemetry_freshness"
      | eval last_seen_telemetry=ts ]
    [ search index=otel_agents earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,agent_host,otel_host,""))))
      | where len(host)>1
      | stats latest(_time) AS ts BY host
      | eval lane="otel_agent_inventory"
      | eval last_seen_telemetry=ts ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd group=tcpin_connections earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(hostname,sourceHost,host,""))))
      | where len(host)>1
      | stats latest(_time) AS ts BY host
      | eval lane="splunk_uf_tcpin"
      | eval last_seen_telemetry=ts ]
    [ | tstats latest(_time) AS ts WHERE (index=aws_cloudtrail OR index=azure_aad OR index=gcp_audit OR index=security) earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY host
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval lane="tstats_cloud_audit_lane"
      | eval last_seen_telemetry=ts ]
| eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,""))))
| where len(host)>1
| sort 0 host - last_seen_telemetry
| streamstats window=2 global=f current=f last(last_seen_telemetry) AS prev_seen BY host
| stats max(last_seen_telemetry) AS last_seen_telemetry values(lane) AS lane_mv max(prev_seen) AS dwell_hint BY host
| eval actual_streams=mvjoin(mvsort(mvdedup(lane_mv)),"|")
| eval stream_dc=mvcount(mvsort(mvdedup(lane_mv)))
| eventstats median(stream_dc) AS fleet_stream_median
| join type=left max=0 host
    [| inputlookup cmdb_master_inventory.csv
     | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,fqdn,primary_dns,""))))
     | fields host host_role host_environment cmdb_class cmdb_owner cmdb_status business_unit compliance_zone geo_region ]
| join type=left max=0 host_role
    [| inputlookup expected_telemetry_streams_by_role.csv
     | eval host_role=lower(trim(toString(host_role)))
     | fields host_role expected_streams min_prod_coverage_pct ]
| eval host_role=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host_role,"unknown_role"))))
| eval host_environment=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host_environment,"unknown"))))
| eval cmdb_class=coalesce(cmdb_class,"")
| eval cmdb_owner=coalesce(cmdb_owner,"")
| eval cmdb_status=coalesce(cmdb_status,"")
| eval business_unit=coalesce(business_unit,"unknown_bu")
| eval compliance_zone=coalesce(compliance_zone,"unknown_zone")
| eval geo_region=coalesce(geo_region,"unknown_region")
| eval in_cmdb=if(len(cmdb_class)>0 OR len(cmdb_owner)>0,1,0)
| eval expected_streams=coalesce(expected_streams,"tstats_telemetry_freshness|splunk_uf_tcpin|otel_agent_inventory")
| eval exp_parts=split(expected_streams,"|")
| eval exp_cnt=mvcount(exp_parts)
| eval coverage_ratio=if(exp_cnt>0, round(100*stream_dc/exp_cnt,2), null())
| eval gap_age_h=if(isnotnull(last_seen_telemetry), round((now()-last_seen_telemetry)/3600,3), null())
| eval gap_type=case(in_cmdb==1 AND (isnull(last_seen_telemetry) OR gap_age_h>24),"blind_spot", in_cmdb==0 AND isnotnull(last_seen_telemetry),"rogue_asset", in_cmdb==1 AND isnotnull(coverage_ratio) AND coverage_ratio<100 AND match(host_environment,"prod|production"),"partial_stream_prod", in_cmdb==1 AND isnotnull(coverage_ratio) AND coverage_ratio<100,"partial_stream_nonprod", true(),"covered_or_review")
| eventstats avg(coverage_ratio) AS env_avg_coverage BY host_environment
| eval severity=case(
    gap_type=="blind_spot" AND match(host_environment,"prod|production"), "critical",
    gap_type=="rogue_asset" AND stream_dc>=2, "high",
    gap_type=="partial_stream_prod", "high",
    gap_type=="blind_spot", "medium",
    gap_type=="partial_stream_nonprod", "low",
    gap_type=="rogue_asset", "medium",
    true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    gap_type=="blind_spot", "restore_agents_forwarders_syslog_or_fix_dns_host_alias_for_inventory_key",
    gap_type=="rogue_asset", "classify_host_in_cmdb_or_quarantine_unauthorized_telemetry_source",
    gap_type=="partial_stream_prod" OR gap_type=="partial_stream_nonprod", "deploy_missing_streams_from_expected_telemetry_streams_by_role",
    true(), "maintain_weekly_inventory_reconciliation_runbook")
| table host host_role host_environment cmdb_class cmdb_owner cmdb_status last_seen_telemetry expected_streams actual_streams coverage_ratio business_unit compliance_zone geo_region severity gap_type recommended_action
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Inventory.dest) AS inv_dest latest(Inventory.vendor_product) AS vendor_product FROM datamodel=Inventory WHERE nodename=Inventory earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Inventory.dest
| rename Inventory.dest AS host
| eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
| join type=left max=0 host [| inputlookup cmdb_master_inventory.csv | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,fqdn,"")))) | fields host cmdb_class cmdb_owner]
| table host vendor_product inv_dest cmdb_class cmdb_owner
```

## Visualization

Severity-colored table by host with drilldowns to multisearch lane_mv, coverage_ratio trend sparklines by host_environment, single-value tiles for env_avg_coverage, and maps or filters by business_unit, compliance_zone, and geo_region.

## Known False Positives

Decommissioned servers often remain marked in_service in CMDB for weeks, producing blind_spot rows until lifecycle automation updates cmdb_status. Development and sandbox hosts are frequently under-instrumented by design; environment-tiered thresholds and min_prod_coverage_pct in the expected_streams lookup prevent false escalations when ratios dip outside production. Stale CMDB owners and business_unit labels route pages to the wrong team until identity refresh jobs run. IP address reuse and DHCP churn make rogue_asset signals ambiguous without IPAM correlation. Hostname normalization mismatches between FQDN and short names split a single machine into two keys until transforms align primary_dns. Containerized workloads may emit telemetry under pod names while CMDB lists nodes; without node_role and orchestration tags, partial_stream detections confuse platform and application owners. Cloud ephemeral instances can appear in telemetry before billing snapshots land, temporarily skewing account-level narratives. Maintenance windows without annotated suppressions still look like outages unless change_ticket_id flows into a suppression lookup. Duplicate forwarders behind NAT may collapse tcpin counts, overstating fleet health. Finally, overly aggressive expected_streams templates flag nearly every host as partial_stream until architects publish realistic baselines per role.
